{{Quote|Subject wanted for the beating death of an off-duty Coruscant Police Officer.|Panza Hondi's bounty posting by the Coruscant Police Force|<ref name="SWBH" />}}
'''Panza Hondi''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Guineo]] criminal living in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]. During the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Hondi worked for [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], a [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] manufacturer that disguised itself as a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packing plant. During his [[Time/Legends|time]] living on Coruscant, Hondi [[Death/Legends|murdered]] a [[Unidentified Coruscant Police officer (Panza Hondi)|police officer]] of the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Police Force]] by beating the [[Police officer/Legends|officer]] to death, which caused the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] to put a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head. While Hondi was working at Haugg Nerf, his bounty was claimed by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who was there [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|looking for]] information about [[Komari Vosa]], the leader of a secret criminal organization known as the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]].

==Biography==
[[File:Duginlikeanalabamatick.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Panza Hondi, murderer]]
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Guineo]] criminal<ref name="SWBH" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|hailing]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dohlban]],<ref name="BHSG" /> Panza Hondi was living in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|II|55|Hondi, Panza}}</ref> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">The events of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' take place in 32 BBY, according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> While living in the Coruscant Industrial District,<ref name="TCSWE" /> Hondi worked for [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], a local [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] distributor disguised as a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packaging plant and owned by an individual named [[Groff Haugg]].<ref name="SWBH" /> By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] 32 BBY,<ref name="NEC" /> Hondi had [[Death/Legends|murdered]] a [[Unidentified Coruscant Police officer (Panza Hondi)|police officer]] of the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Police Force]] while the [[Police officer/Legends|officer]] was not on duty. In response to the death of their [[Officer/Legends|officer]], the Coruscant Police Force placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Hondi with his last known location being the Industrial District, promising a reward of 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for live capture and 1,000 credits for his death.<ref name="SWBH" />

While he worked for Haugg Nerf, Hondi was affiliated with a number of thugs-for-hire.<ref name="SWBH" /> During the same year,<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|infiltrated]] Haugg Nerf Industries while searching for information to locate [[Komari Vosa]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the leader of a secretive criminal cult known as the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]], who were rumored to be behind an increase in the demand for death sticks.<ref name="SWBH" /> During Fett's infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries, the bounty hunter apprehended Hondi<ref name="TCSWE" /> on one of the facility's walkways.<ref name="BHSG" />
{{Gamemechanics}}
Fett claimed the bounty placed on Hondi, capturing him alive.<ref name="SWBH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Like many other Guineo, Panza Hondi had [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] skin of a [[Color/Legends|pale yellow]] color,<ref name="SWBH" /> and was over two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="BHSG" /> A known affiliate of many different hired thugs,<ref name="SWBH" /> the violent and temperamental<ref name="BHSG" /> Hondi was not above murdering [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings,<ref name="TCSWE" /> as shown when he beat a Coruscant Police officer to death. He also had some knowledge about using a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="SWBH" />

==Equipment==
While working for Haugg Nerf Industries, Panza Hondi carried a blaster that fired red [[Laser/Legends|energy bolts]]. He wore a yellow [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] with a gray plate on its front that had two tubes attached to it, each of which connected to one of the two silver-colored tanks attached to the back of his suit. Hondi's jumpsuit also had white stripes along the sleeves and around the collar. Along with the jumpsuit, Hondi wore light-gray [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], dark-gray [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a pair of gray [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]].<ref name="SWBH" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BountyHunter-Cover.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Panza Hondi made his first appearance in ''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter''.]]
Panza Hondi first appeared in the [[2002]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' as an optional secondary bounty in Coruscant's Industrial District.<ref name="SWBH" /> Hondi is the first bounty in the district and is the game's forty first bounty overall.<ref name="BHSG" />

In [[2008]], Panza Hondi received an entry in [[Del Rey]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which established that Jango Fett apprehended him but did not say whether Hondi was killed or not.<ref name="TCSWE" /> In ''Bounty Hunter'', the player controls Jango Fett, and, since claiming Hondi's bounty is not essential to the game, the player has several ways to approach the bounty. The player can ignore Hondi entirely, kill him and not claim the bounty, kill him and claim the bounty, or capture him and claim the bounty.<ref name="SWBH" /> ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' established his capture,<ref name="TCSWE" /> so he must have been captured dead or alive. This article was written with the assumption that the player chose to capture Hondi alive, since that resulted in Jango Fett receiving the maximum reward possible.<ref name="SWBH" />
